name: ESSAI 2026
venue: European Summer School on Artificial Intelligence, Vienna, Austria
description: The 4th European Summer School on Artificial Intelligence (ESSAI) gathers PhD students and young researchers for a week‑long intensive program in Vienna, Austria. It offers introductory and advanced courses across all AI sub‑disciplines, along with specialized tutorials, networking opportunities, and a rich social program. The school aims to provide a broad view of AI, foster collaborations, and connect participants with world‑renowned speakers and industry challenges.

name: OpenEuroLLM Winter School
description: The 2026 Winter School on Multilinguality in LLM Development and Evaluation is held at the Skeikampen conference hotel north of Oslo, Norway. It targets NLP researchers, especially those from EuroHPC consortium countries and doctoral students, offering technical presentations, hands‑on elements, and discussions on multilingual large language models and evaluation. The program includes expert talks, a walk‑through of EuroHPC experience reports, and networking opportunities, with travel and accommodation subsidized by the OpenEuroLLM project.

name: Probabilistic AI School
description: The Probabilistic AI School 2026 will be held in Vilnius, Lithuania from 3‑7 August 2026. It aims to provide an inclusive educational environment featuring state‑of‑the‑art machine learning and artificial intelligence expertise. The school welcomes enthusiasts, students, academics, and industry professionals to learn and collaborate.

name: BMVA Computer Vision Summer School
description: The 29th BMVA Computer Vision Summer School will be held at Durham University in Durham, England, UK. It offers an intensive week of lectures and lab sessions covering a wide range of topics in computer vision, delivered by leading researchers from the UK and abroad. The programme features daily oral talks and hands‑on sessions designed for students and early‑career researchers seeking advanced training in the field.

name: Oxford Machine Learning Summer School 2026
description: OxML Summer 2026 offers participants the chance to upskill in the latest AI theory and applications through three specialised tracks. The programme features world‑renowned experts from frontier AI labs in both academia and industry, providing a unique and customisable learning experience. Attendees can choose any combination of tracks to match their individual learning goals.

name: Robotics, Perception and Learning (RPL) Summer School
venue: KTH Royal Institute of Technology, Stockholm, Sweden
description: The RPL Summer School 2026 is a six‑day event organized by the Department for Robotics, Perception and Learning at KTH Royal Institute of Technology in Stockholm, Sweden. It brings together doctoral and postdoctoral researchers to discuss topics such as machine learning, computer vision, robotics, human‑robot interaction, planning and decision making through keynotes, industry talks and brainstorming sessions. The program includes student presentations, indoor and outdoor ice‑breaker activities, and aims to foster new collaborations and concrete research ideas.

name: Machine Learning Crash Course at MALGA
venue: Università di Genova, Via Dodecaneso 35, Genova, Italy
description: The MLCC2026 Summer School – Machine Learning Crash Course – is a five‑day intensive program held at the University of Genoa’s DIBRIS/DIMA building in Genova, Italy. It provides an introduction to fundamental machine‑learning concepts and algorithms, combining theoretical lectures with practical lab sessions for undergraduate, graduate students and professionals. The course is taught in person, includes hands‑on notebook exercises, and offers university credit upon successful completion.

name: UK Robotics Summer School
venue: National Robotarium, Heriot-Watt University, Edinburgh, United Kingdom
description: The 2026 UK Robotics Summer School is a five‑day program held at the National Robotarium, Heriot‑Watt University in Edinburgh, United Kingdom. It offers expert‑led lectures, tutorials and hands‑on sessions covering bio‑inspired robotics, healthcare robotics, generative AI for robotics, safe AI, and real‑world applications, aimed at master’s‑level students and industry practitioners. Participants will also benefit from networking opportunities with academics and industry leaders, as well as a special dinner and other refreshments.

name: International Summer School for Advanced Studies on Biometrics, Behavior, and Vision
description: The 2026 International Summer School on Biometrics takes place in Alghero, Sardinia, Italy, hosted at the Hotel Dei Pini. It focuses on the impact of generative AI and large foundation models on biometric and human‑recognition technologies, covering machine learning, image understanding, signal analysis, neuroscience, robotics, forensic and digital forensics. The program is aimed at about 70 pre‑selected PhD students, post‑docs, researchers and professionals, offering lectures by 20 leading experts, hands‑on sessions, and optional scholarships.

name: 1st Internationa School on the Cognitive, Ethical, and Societal Dimensions of AI - AIces
description: AIces 2026 is the first International School focusing on the cognitive, ethical and societal dimensions of artificial intelligence, offering multidisciplinary discussions on AI foundations, challenges and risks. The programme is aimed at graduate and postgraduate students as well as industry practitioners, with no formal academic prerequisites. It features two parallel courses, optional symposium and open‑debate sessions, live online participation, and recordings available for 45 days after the event.

name: Winter School on Ethical, Legal, and Societal (ELS) aspects of AI and AS at Umeå University
description: The Winter School on Ethical, Legal, and Societal aspects of AI and Autonomous Systems is held at Umeå University in Sweden. It offers lectures, hands‑on multidisciplinary training, a poster session and a social program aimed at networking and professional exchange. The school targets researchers, students and professionals interested in the societal implications of AI and AS, providing practical insights and discussions.

name: Winter School on Social Robotics, Artificial Intelligence and Multimedia (SoRAIM 2026)
description: The Winter School on Social Robotics, Artificial Intelligence and Multimedia (SoRAIM) brings together researchers from fields such as computer vision, speech/audio processing, and human‑robot interaction. It aims to foster cross‑disciplinary exchange through formal courses, expert talks, and hands‑on sessions in a scenic mountain setting near Grenoble. Participants can benefit from top‑level speakers covering topics like affective signal processing, conversational speech synthesis, and multimodal dialogue, while also enjoying informal networking and outdoor activities.

name: Machine Learning Summer School (MLSS) Melbourne
venue: Sandridge Events Centre, Port Melbourne, Australia
description: The 2026 Machine Learning Summer School (MLSS) takes place in Melbourne, Australia at the Sandridge Events Centre. It offers a two‑week, in‑person program from 2 to 13 February for 50 hand‑picked PhD students and early‑career researchers, covering core theory and emerging topics beyond large language models. World‑class speakers from academia and industry present lectures and tutorials on deep learning, generative modeling, reinforcement learning, and other cutting‑edge areas.

name: Northern Lights Deep Learning Winter School
venue: UiT The Arctic University of Norway, Tromsø, Norway
description: The 2027 Winter School is part of the Northern Lights Deep Learning Conference and will be held in Tromsø, Norway. It focuses on deep learning topics such as image classification, object detection, segmentation, time‑series prediction and speech recognition. The school aims to bring together national and international researchers to exchange ideas, foster collaborations, and present cutting‑edge research.

name: Annual Nepal AI School (ANAIS)
description: The Sixth Annual Nepal AI School (ANAIS) is an 11‑day intensive program held at Swostishree Gurukul in Sanobharyang, Kathmandu, Nepal. It offers a comprehensive curriculum that spans AI fundamentals, advanced methods, and emerging applications such as computer vision, natural language processing, and AI for health. The school targets senior undergraduates, graduate students, PhD candidates, faculty members, and industry professionals, providing lectures, hands‑on labs, and project work to foster research collaboration.

name: EEML Summer School
description: The Eastern European Machine Learning Summer School takes place in Cetinje, Montenegro from 27 July to 1 August 2026. It offers a program of lectures and tutorials delivered by leading researchers from institutions such as Google DeepMind, MIT, University of Oxford and many others. The school is aimed at students and early‑career researchers interested in machine‑learning topics, and applications are accepted until 31 March 2026.

name: Mediterranean Summer School
venue: University of Split Faculty of Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering and Naval Architecture, Split, Croatia
description: The Mediterranean Machine Learning (M2L) Summer School provides a five‑day program of keynotes, lectures, and hands‑on sessions led by international AI experts. It targets doctoral and outstanding master students, as well as academics and practitioners, especially from the Mediterranean region, and encourages participants to present their work in poster sessions. The school also emphasizes networking through social activities and aims to promote diversity, inclusion, and collaboration across the machine‑learning community.

name: Summer School on Multimodal Foundation Models and Generative AI
venue: Institut National des Postes et Télécommunications, Rabat, Morocco
description: The 2nd edition of the AI Summer School on Multimodal Foundation Models and Generative AI is a five‑day immersive program jointly organized by MoroccoAI, INPT, IMT Nord Europe, and Soft Centre. It targets master and Ph.D. students, post‑docs, young and senior researchers, and industry professionals, offering theoretical lectures, hands‑on labs, and networking opportunities. The school aims to equip participants with cutting‑edge AI knowledge, practical skills, and leadership experience to advance Morocco's AI ecosystem.

name: AthNLP Summer School 2026
description: The Athens Natural Language Processing Summer School 2026 will be held at the NCSR Demokritos campus in Athens, Greece. The program, organized by RC Athena, NCSR Demokritos, Athens University of Economics and Business, and Heriot‑Watt University, focuses on advanced topics in natural language processing. It runs from 2 to 8 September and is scheduled to precede the 14th EETN Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Chania.

name: Advanced Artificial Intelligence: GenAI & Deep Unsupervised Learning
venue: Lady Margaret Hall, Oxford, United Kingdom
description: The Advanced Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning summer school at Lady Margaret Hall focuses on generative AI and deep unsupervised learning techniques. It combines theoretical lectures with hands‑on projects covering clustering, GANs, self‑supervised learning, anomaly detection, and applications across various domains. The programme is aimed at STEM students with intermediate AI/ML experience who are considering graduate study or research.

name: AI4Science Summer School
description: The 2nd Summer School on Advancing Scientific Discovery with AI is organized in Caen, Normandy, France and targets researchers interested in AI‑driven science. It covers machine learning for data analysis, AI‑guided experimental design, generative models, and integration of AI with simulations, featuring hands‑on workshops and case studies led by experts. The program is aimed at PhD students, post‑doctoral researchers and scientists with experience in mathematical modelling, numerical computation and Python programming.

name: LUT Summer School
description: LUT Summer School is a one‑week academic event offering intensive courses in sustainability, climate action, marketing, management, inventive thinking and the intersection of business and technology. The programme takes place on the Lappeenranta campus of LUT University in Finland from 3 – 7 August 2026 and combines classroom teaching, workshops, group work and social activities in Finnish nature. It is aimed at bachelor’s and master’s students from any university, with courses worth 3 ECTS each and an application deadline of 31 May 2026.

name: Armenian LLM Summer School 2025
description: The LLM Summer School 2026 is an intensive program for students and researchers focusing on the latest advancements in Large Language Models. It will be held in Yerevan, Armenia and will feature a combination of lectures, hands‑on workshops, and a hackathon where participants can apply their knowledge to innovative projects. The event is part of an annual series that aims to provide comprehensive training and networking opportunities in the field of LLMs.

name: Armenian LLM Summer School 2025
description: The Armenia LLM Summer School 2026 is a five‑day intensive program on Large Language Models held at the AI9 Startup Campus in Yerevan, Armenia. It combines in‑person lectures, hands‑on workshops, and an online Zoom track, culminating in a 24‑hour hackathon on August 8. The school targets motivated AI students, researchers and engineers, offering practical experience with GPU resources and financial aid options.
